title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Miss Bunner, a housekeeper, confides in Miss Marple about her suspicions regarding Patrick, a relative of Miss Blacklock. Miss Bunner expresses gratitude toward Lotte for her kindness and mentions that Miss Blacklock promised her a pension and furniture in her will. Miss Bunner believes Miss Blacklock is too trusting and notes that Patrick has twice asked her for money by feigning debt. Miss Bunner suspects Patrick may be morally responsible for the suicide of a Swiss young man and believes he may have been involved in the recent "terrible business."

Miss Bunner shares two specific observations: she once caught Patrick in the shrubbery holding a greasy cup, and she overheard him arguing with Julia. During that argument, Patrick claimed he was warning Julia against a black market deal, though Miss Bunner doubts this. She also suspects Patrick tampered with a table lamp in the drawing room. Miss Blacklock interrupts the conversation, prompting Miss Bunner to hastily change the subject.

After Miss Blacklock and Miss Bunce depart, Round asks what was discussed. Miss Marple reflects on the power of family ties, citing a case where a daughter protected her father from a poisoning charge. Miss Marple then engages Round in conversation about a former parlour maid named Miss Florence, noting that the maid was easily attracted to polite men.
